name: Agauos
body text: A noble child is born to Paris and Helen after a pitstop on rocky Kranae. Can only be played when Paris possesses a Helen. Agauos remains attached to Paris thenceforth. If Paris possess the real Helen at end of game, he gains an additional 50 Fame if Willing, but loses 50 if Unwilling.

name: Amphikalypsis
body text: One palace becomes hidden to all other players. Cannot be entered until another player reveals by spending three Oracle tokens. Mark hidden city with Maze marker shown above.

name: Becalmed
body text: Interrupts one player’s Voyage at moment played (player loses remaining MPs). If still at a Palace, player may undergo Hospitality.

name: Divine Patroness
body text: Immediately cancel or cause storm in any sea (draw Voyage card for Storm Strength/Swept To location). OR Change a Helen’s Willingness. OR +5/-5 to any Voyage card. OR +2d6 to any one roll by Hero

name: Escape
body text: A Shipwrecked/Captured Hero gets new ship and escapes to adjacent Sea Circle. OR Cancel a Pirates card. OR Cancel Intercept card. OR Unwilling Helen gets new ship and moves immediately

name: Expert Helmsman
body text: -2d6 on all Storms. Can only be played while Hero is at a Palace

name: Cypriot Fleet
body text: Can only be played by Hero allied with Paphos. +5d6 to any one Sack City attempt or Naval Engagement (over multiple rounds). Discard after use.

name: Egyptian Fleet
body text: Can be played by any Hero with an Allied palace in Egypt. Adds d6 x Palace Rank to one Sack City attempt or Naval Engagement (over multiple rounds). Discard after use.

name: Hittite Fleet
body text: Can be played by any Hero with an Allied palace in Hatti. Adds d6 x Palace Rank to one Sack City attempt or Naval Engagement (over multiple rounds). Discard after use.

name: Phoenician Fleet
body text: Can be played by any Hero with an Allied palace in Phoenicia. Adds d6 x Palace Rank to one Sack City attempt or Naval Engagement (over multiple rounds). Discard after use.

name: Syrian Fleet
body text: Can be played by any Hero with an Allied palace in Syria. Adds d6 x Palace Rank to one Sack City attempt or Naval Engagement (over multiple rounds). Discard after use.

name: Help from Eidothea
body text: Automatic succeed wrestling and escaping Proteus OR Shipwrecked Hero gets new ship and escapes to adjacent Sea Circle OR Cancel Becalmed OR +2MP on one Voyage

name: Hermes
body text: Choose up to two existing Helens to examine. You may swap them.

name: Intercept
body text: Allied palace may attempt to Intercept passing Hero. Roll d6 = Palace Rank – 1d6 for each Sea CIRCLE away from the Palace. On a success proceed to Naval Engagement. If the Hero has no Sea Peoples or Fleets

name: Man Overboard
body text: Play during a Storm. Forces player with an Expert Helmsman, or other expert to discard them. OR player loses ten crew

name: March of Mopsos
body text: Resolve immediately. Series of Sack Palace attempts beginning from from Awarna and proceeding clockwise along the coast to Ura

name: Master Shipwork
body text: +2 MPs to all Voyages. Cannot be played on existing ship. Play when receiving new ship

name: New Foundation
body text: Play at any sacked Palace. Roll 1d6 for every 10 points of Sea Peoples played (including any surviving from immediately preceding Sack Palace attempt). Each success contributes to New Foundation Rank. The site is Allied to founding player

name: Night Attack
body text: +4d6 on the first round of a Sack Palace attempt. Can only be played after undergoing normal Hospitality (not Host Attacks) at Neutral, Allied, or Enemy Allied Palace.

name: Cypriot Pirates
body text: Forces immediate Ship Melee with Hero mid-voyage. Pirate strength 5d6 reduced by 1d6 for each sea AREA away from home territory +2 Fame for every step of Pirates eliminated.

name: Libyan Pirates
body text: Forces immediate Ship Melee with Hero mid-voyage. Pirate strength 5d6 reduced by 1d6 for each sea AREA away from home territory +2 Fame for every step of Pirates eliminated.

name: Lukka Pirates
body text: Forces immediate Ship Melee with Hero mid-voyage. Pirate strength 5d6 reduced by 1d6 for each sea AREA away from home territory +2 Fame for every step of Pirates eliminated.

name: Phoenician Pirates
body text: Forces immediate Ship Melee with Hero mid-voyage. Pirate strength 5d6 reduced by 1d6 for each sea AREA away from home territory  +2 Fame for every step of Pirates eliminated.

name: Cyrian Pirates
body text: Forces immediate Ship Melee with Hero mid-voyage. Pirate strength 5d6 reduced by 1d6 for each sea AREA away from home territory +2 Fame for every step of Pirates eliminated.

name: Sack Palace
body text: Roll trial combat. Need successes = Palace Rank in single throw. If unsuccessful, Palace counterattacks with d6 x Palace Rank; Hero may then retreat one Sea Circle, or attack again. If successful, place Sacked Palace marker, take Slave counters = Palace Rank, Gifts = Palace Rank x 3, Fame = 10 x Palace Rank (if Palace was Allied to you, lose same amount).

name: Sea Peoples Danuna
body text: 1d6 to one Naval Engagement or Sack Palace attempt each round. OR 1d6 to New Foundation attempt (2.6). Discard after use.

name: Sea Peoples Peleset
body text: 2d6 to one Naval Engagement or Sack Palace attempt

name: Sea Peoples Shaklusha
body text: 1d6 to one Naval Engagement or Sack Palace attempt

name: Sea Peoples Shardana
body text: 2d6 to one Naval Engagement or Sack Palace attempt

name: Sea Peoples Tjekkeru
body text: 2d6 to one Naval Engagement or Sack Palace attempt

name: Sea Peoples Washash
body text: 1d6 to one Naval Engagement or Sack Palace attempt

name: Sea Peoples Mixed
body text: 1d6 to one Naval Engagement or Sack Palace attempt

name: Smooth Sailing
body text: Cancel any one Storm (including from Divine Patroness) AND All Sea Circles and Coastal Circles in that Sea cost 2 MP and 1 MP respectively for you this turn

name: Terracotta Fleet
body text: Causes Cypriot Fleet (only) to disband.

name: Threats
body text: B46+4d6 to one Diplomacy attempt. If diplomacy is unsuccessful, the Palace becomes Hostile; lose 5 Fame x Palace Rank and move to adjacent Sea Circle.

name: Trial Combat
body text: Iinitiates ritual combat with another Hero, that you are in the same location as. The opposing Hero may decline at the cost of 5 Fame x current HPs, which you gain.

name: Tritons Favor
body text: +4 MPs to any one Voyage. OR 8d6 against any Pirates card (once). OR 8d6 against another Hero in during a Naval conflict. OR -1d6 from Intercept attempt. OR Cancel Man Overboard.

name: Will of Zeus
body text: Cancel any Theme card. OR look at a Theme card from another players hand and take it (player draws new card). OR + 4d6 any roll.

name: Disguise
body text: Hero can visit Enemy Palace for all normal purposes (Hospitality, Oracles, Helen)

name: Shifting Wind
body text: Player may rotate any voyage card 45 degrees for desired MP values

name: Unseasonable Wind
body text: Search Voyage deck for first card of any later season. Swap or not with top card of deck, and play as Voyage Card for this turn..

name: Night Voyage
body text: Play during other player's turn. Hero and ship move instantly to any other palace on same sea (suffering storm damage if applicable). Night Voyage can be used to avoid Trial Combat. Cannot be played to and from Thebes.

name: Feint
body text: Reroll any one trial combat round. OR redo your movement for a turn in response to a pirate or capture card. You regain all movement points, and may still move through the affected sea area, but will still suffer the effects if you do so.

name: First Strike
body text: Roll first in trial combat where you are not the aggressor OR roll first in a host attacks hostpilality card

name: Inspiration
body text: you must discard two cards, then draw three

name: Rumors
body text: Add or Remove any visited token, you gain five fame

name: Deflect
body text: Redicect enemy hero's attack damage in trial combat against them. OR if an opponent plays a pirate card on you, instead take that card into your hand, you may play it later
